system_metadata.description: Top CARC/RARC combinations for automated 835 remittance explanation
system_metadata.usage_instruction: Use 'code' as the primary key for lookup when parsing 835 CAS segments.

CARC_definitions.title: Deductible Amount
CARC_definitions.description: The amount applied to the patient's annual deductible. The provider should collect this from the patient.
CARC_definitions.action: Verify patient's remaining deductible and bill the patient directly.

CARC_definitions.title: Coinsurance Amount
CARC_definitions.description: The percentage of the allowed amount that is the patient's responsibility.
CARC_definitions.action: Bill the patient for the coinsurance percentage defined in their plan.

CARC_definitions.title: Incomplete Claim
CARC_definitions.description: Claim/service lacks information or has submission/billing error(s).
CARC_definitions.action: Check the associated RARC code (e.g., N211) to see exactly what data is missing and resubmit.

CARC_definitions.title: Duplicate Claim
CARC_definitions.description: This is an exact duplicate of a claim or service that was previously processed.
CARC_definitions.action: Do not resubmit. Check previous remittances for the original payment or denial.

CARC_definitions.title: Coordination of Benefits (COB)
CARC_definitions.description: This care may be covered by another payer per coordination of benefits rules.
CARC_definitions.action: Identify the primary insurance carrier and submit the claim to them first.

CARC_definitions.title: Coverage Not Active
CARC_definitions.description: Expenses were incurred before the patient's insurance coverage was active.
CARC_definitions.action: Verify the patient's effective date of coverage and check if the date of service was correct.

CARC_definitions.title: Contractual Obligation
CARC_definitions.description: Charge exceeds the maximum allowed or contracted fee arrangement between the provider and payer.
CARC_definitions.action: This is usually a mandatory write-off based on the provider's contract with the insurer.

CARC_definitions.title: Non-covered Charges
CARC_definitions.description: These charges are for services that are statutorily excluded or not covered by the plan.
CARC_definitions.action: Check the plan documents. The patient may be liable if an ABN or notice was signed.

CARC_definitions.title: Wrong Payer
CARC_definitions.description: Claim not covered by this payer. You must send it to the correct contractor.
CARC_definitions.action: Verify the payer ID on the patient's insurance card and resubmit to the correct entity.

RARC_definitions.title: Benefit Restrictions
RARC_definitions.description: Consult plan benefit documents/guidelines for information about restrictions for this service.
RARC_definitions.action: Review the specific medical policy for this procedure on the payer's portal.

RARC_definitions.title: Local Coverage Determination (LCD)
RARC_definitions.description: This decision was based on a local coverage determination (LCD).
RARC_definitions.action: Ensure the diagnosis code used (ICD-10) is listed as 'medically necessary' in the payer's LCD list.

RARC_definitions.title: Missing Plan Info
RARC_definitions.description: Missing plan information for other insurance.
RARC_definitions.action: The patient has other active insurance. Update the 'Other Payer' section in the 837 file.

RARC_definitions.title: Timely Filing Expired
RARC_definitions.description: The time limit for filing this claim has expired.
RARC_definitions.action: Check if there was an exception or delay; otherwise, this claim is uncollectible.
